name: CASEY CHAN BLDG E2
address: 62968 OB RILEY ROAD BLDG E2, BEND, OR 97701

fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Improving emergency response effectiveness on this property could involve creating and maintaining clear and defined access routes for emergency vehicles to reach all parts of the property, especially areas with currently limited access. Ensuring pedestrian walkways are kept unobstructed and wide enough to accommodate emergency personnel and equipment may assist in responder movement and facilitate efficient operations. Strategically managing the dense vegetation within the landscaped and open green areas could enhance visibility for responders, allowing them to swiftly locate critical areas. Establishing alternative access options at the rear of the main building may offer additional entry points, supporting better staging and movement flexibility for emergency teams.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Scattered Shrubs
wildfire.recommendations: To enhance wildfire preparedness, managing vegetation adjacent to the structure by creating clear separation can reduce potential fire exposure and improve defensible space. Pruning and strategically thinning dense tree clusters could decrease fuel continuity, while maintaining scattered trees and shrubs in a way that ensures spacing between crowns can lower the chances of fire spread. Preserving and expanding open areas around the property could further enhance defensible space and long-term resilience. Maintaining these open areas will also support safe firefighter access and improve the effectiveness of suppression operations. Regular vegetation management and strategic clearance around the structure and access routes can fortify the property's defense against wildfire threats.
